When Israel was weak and helpless like children, foolish and disobedient like children, then God loved them; he supported them as a nurse supports a baby, nourished them, and tolerated their behavior. All who are grown up should often reflect on the goodness of God to them in their childhood. He took care of them, worked with them, not only as a father or a tutor, but as a mother or nurse. When they were in the wilderness, God showed them the way they should go and supported them, taking them by the arms. He taught them the way of his commands through the ceremonial law given by Moses. He took them by the arms to guide them so that they would not stray, and to hold them up so that they would not stumble and fall. God’s spiritual Israel are all thus supported. It is God's work to draw poor souls to himself; and no one can come to him unless he draws them. With bands of love—this word signifies stronger cords than the former. He eased them of the burdens they had long groaned under. Israel is very ungrateful to God. God’s plans would have saved them, but their own plans ruined them. They backslide; there is no stability in them, no steadfastness. They turn away from me, from God, the greatest good. They are inclined to backslide; they are ready to sin; they eagerly embrace every temptation. Their hearts are fully set on doing evil. Only those who are truly happy are taught by the Lord through his Spirit, upheld by his power, and caused to walk in his ways. By his grace he takes away the love and control of sin, and creates a desire for the blessed feast of the gospel, that they may feed on it and live forever.

God is slow to anger, and is reluctant to abandon a people to complete ruin who have been called by his name. When God was about to give a sacrifice for sin, and a Savior for sinners, he did not spare his own Son, so that he might spare us. This is the language of the day of his patience; but when people turn away from this, then the great day of his wrath comes. Human compassion is nothing in comparison with the tender mercies of our God, whose thoughts and ways, in receiving returning sinners, are as much above ours as heaven is above the earth. God knows how to pardon poor sinners. He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and in doing so declares his righteousness, now that Christ has purchased the pardon, and he has promised it. Holy fear of the word of Christ will draw us to him, not drive us from him; children tremble and flee to him. And all who come at the gospel call shall have a place and a name in the gospel church. The religious services of Israel were mere hypocrisy, but in Judah regard was had for God’s laws, and the people followed their pious ancestors. Let us be faithful: those who thus honor God, he will honor, but those who despise him will be lightly esteemed.
